{"id":"project-LICHEN-ahp7","title":"EDHOC: Stack buffers containing keys not wiped in all code paths","description":"SECURITY: Several functions have local stack buffers containing cryptographic material that are not wiped in all exit paths.

Locations:

1. edhoc_initiator_process_msg2 (lines 470-731):
- plaintext_2[128] (line 570) - decrypted message, not wiped
- keystream_2[128] (line 559) - key stream, not wiped
- mac_2[32] (line 608) - MAC, not wiped
- sig_struct_2[256] (line 614) - not wiped

2. edhoc_responder_process_msg1 (lines 810-1021):
- plaintext_2[128] (line 942) - not wiped
- keystream_2[128] (line 956) - not wiped
- mac_2[32] (line 928) - not wiped
- sig_struct_2[256] (line 935) - not wiped
- ciphertext_2[128] (line 963) - not wiped

3. edhoc_responder_process_msg3 (lines 1023-1146):
- plaintext_3[128] (line 1066) - contains ID and signature, not wiped
- mac_3[32] (line 1105) - not wiped
- sig_struct_3[256] (line 1111) - not wiped

Impact: Cryptographic material may persist on stack after function returns, potentially leaked via:
- Stack reuse by subsequent functions
- Core dumps
- Memory scanning attacks

Fix: Add crypto_wipe() for all sensitive local buffers before return in both success and error paths.

File: lichen/subsys/lichen/edhoc/edhoc.c:570,559,608,614,942,956,928,935,963,1066,1105,1111","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:46:55.960017Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:46:55.960045Z"}
